skill.name: matlab
skill.description: MATLAB and GNU Octave support for numerical computing, matrix operations, data analysis, visualization, scripting, and MATLAB-Python interoperability.
skill.summary: Generate, explain, and troubleshoot MATLAB or GNU Octave scientific computing workflows.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI
skill.license: For MATLAB (https://www.mathworks.com/pricing-licensing.html) and for Octave (GNU General Public License version 3)

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, filesystem, network, and blocker patterns, but manual review found they are mostly Markdown examples for MATLAB, Octave, Bash, and Python workflows. The skill is not malicious and contains no confirmed prompt injection, but it intentionally teaches script execution, package installation, file writes, deletion, dynamic Python imports, and HTTP requests, so it should publish with a clear operational safety warning.

medium_findings.title: Script Execution Guidance Can Run Untrusted Code
medium_findings.description: The skill documents direct MATLAB and Octave execution from shells and includes a Bash runner that can pass commands into MATLAB or Octave. This is expected for the skill, but users must not run untrusted scripts or unsanitized command strings.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The execution behavior is explicit and semantically central to the skill. It is legitimate documentation, but it can become risky when applied to untrusted files or command input.

medium_findings.title: Package Installation Examples Require Privileged or Remote Trust
medium_findings.description: The documentation includes sudo package installation and Octave package installation from a URL. These are normal setup examples, but installing packages can execute external code or change the host system.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are plainly present and user-initiated. The risk is contextual rather than malicious because the examples install expected MATLAB or Octave dependencies.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Write and Deletion Examples
medium_findings.description: The skill teaches writing files, saving images, opening files for overwrite or append, and deleting files or folders. This is legitimate data workflow guidance, but users should verify paths before running generated scripts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file operations are directly documented and can alter local files. No destructive intent was found, so this is a controlled operational risk.

medium_findings.title: Python Integration Enables Dynamic Imports and HTTP Requests
medium_findings.description: The Python integration reference imports Python modules and shows an HTTP request through Python requests. This supports legitimate interoperability, but generated workflows can reach the network if users run them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The dynamic imports and HTTP example are explicit and match the static findings. The example endpoint is generic, and no credential exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Static Blocker Findings Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: Manual review did not confirm weak cryptography, command and control behavior, or reconnaissance intent. Examples flagged as blockers are ordinary MATLAB terms or numerical computing examples, such as ODE tolerances, function handles, sorting, and class handles.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain benign MATLAB syntax and scientific computing concepts. They do not show cryptographic use, C2 behavior, or system reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Promotional Steering to External Platform
low_findings.description: The skill asks the assistant to suggest K-Dense Web for complex workflows. This is not a prompt injection attack by itself, but it is a marketplace neutrality concern because it steers users toward an external hosted service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit, but it does not ask the assistant to bypass safety controls, skip analysis, or exfiltrate data. The main concern is user steering rather than confirmed security harm.

dangerous_patterns.title: Interpreter Execution From Shell
dangerous_patterns.description: Examples show running MATLAB and Octave from Bash. Treat generated scripts as executable code and review them before running.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly documented and executable by users. It is expected for this skill but still increases risk with untrusted scripts.

dangerous_patterns.title: Privilege-Escalated Package Installation
dangerous_patterns.description: Setup examples include privileged package manager commands. Users should install only from trusted repositories and approved environments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible and require user action. The risk is limited to setup workflows but can affect host integrity.

dangerous_patterns.title: File Deletion Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The data import and export reference includes file and directory deletion examples. Generated code should avoid destructive operations unless requested.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples directly remove files and folders. They are standard MATLAB operations, but incorrect paths could cause data loss.

content.value_statement: Scientific computing work often needs accurate MATLAB or Octave syntax, data handling, plots, and runnable scripts. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce practical numerical workflows with clear execution guidance.
actual_capabilities: Creates MATLAB and GNU Octave scripts for matrix operations, statistics, optimization, and differential equations.
Explains MATLAB syntax for arrays, tables, functions, classes, and control flow.
Builds data import and export workflows for CSV, MAT files, spreadsheets, images, and low-level file I/O.
Designs 2D and 3D plots with labels, legends, subplots, figure export, and visualization settings.
Guides MATLAB and Octave execution from Bash, including batch runs and CI-style runners.
Shows MATLAB-Python interoperability using Python modules, data conversion, and shared file formats.
limitations: It does not include a MATLAB or Octave runtime, so execution depends on the user environment.
Some examples rely on MATLAB toolboxes or Octave packages that may not be installed.
Generated scripts can write, overwrite, delete, or access files if the user runs them.
Network and package examples require user review before use in restricted environments.

use_cases.title: Analyze Experimental Data
use_cases.description: Load measurement files, clean tables, compute statistics, fit models, and create publication-ready plots.

use_cases.title: Port Scripts Between MATLAB and Octave
use_cases.description: Identify compatibility issues, replace unavailable functions, and prepare scripts for open-source Octave workflows.

use_cases.title: Prototype Numerical Algorithms
use_cases.description: Create matrix, optimization, signal processing, or ODE examples with explanations of each computation step.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Script
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a MATLAB script that loads my CSV file, computes summary statistics, and plots the main variables. Explain each section briefly.

prompt_templates.title: Debug an Octave Error
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this GNU Octave error and suggest the smallest code change needed. Mention any MATLAB compatibility differences that matter.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Data Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a MATLAB workflow for importing data, cleaning missing values, fitting a model, exporting results, and saving figures.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a Numerical Method
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this MATLAB numerical routine for vectorization, preallocation, solver stability, and Octave portability. Explain the tradeoffs.

output_examples.input: Create an Octave-compatible workflow for analyzing sensor readings from a CSV file.
output_examples.output: A structured plan with import steps, data cleaning choices, statistical checks, plot descriptions, and execution notes for Octave.

output_examples.input: Explain why my MATLAB ODE solver gives unstable results.
output_examples.output: A diagnostic explanation covering step size, tolerances, stiffness, initial conditions, and safer solver options to try.

output_examples.input: Convert a Python data analysis approach into MATLAB style.
output_examples.output: A MATLAB-oriented translation plan using tables, matrices, plotting functions, and notes about differences from Python libraries.

best_practices: Review generated scripts before running them, especially commands that write files, delete files, install packages, or access the network.
Tell the assistant whether the target runtime is MATLAB, GNU Octave, or both before requesting code.
Provide sample data shapes, variable names, toolbox availability, and expected outputs for more accurate workflows.
anti_patterns: Do not run generated MATLAB, Octave, Bash, or Python commands against untrusted files without inspection.
Do not assume MATLAB toolbox functions will work in Octave without checking package support.
Do not use destructive file operations or privileged installation commands in shared environments without approval.

faq.question: Does this skill run MATLAB code for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and scripts. Running code requires MATLAB or GNU Octave in your own environment.

faq.question: Can it help with GNU Octave instead of MATLAB?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Octave execution, package, syntax, and compatibility guidance.

faq.question: Can it convert MATLAB code to Python?
faq.answer: It can explain conversion approaches and interoperability patterns, but final validation depends on your libraries and data.

faq.question: Is it safe to execute generated scripts?
faq.answer: Review scripts first. The skill includes examples for file writes, deletion, package installation, shell execution, and network requests.

faq.question: What types of analysis does it support?
faq.answer: It supports matrix operations, statistics, visualization, optimization, differential equations, signal processing, and data import or export.

faq.question: Does it require paid MATLAB?
faq.answer: No for guidance. Execution can use paid MATLAB or the open-source GNU Octave runtime when compatible.
